description:
With .6.3 in place, the trampoline only dispatches at: program entry, parking-reachable callee returns, closure-invoke returns, and resume-after-park. The general "while !cur.is_null() { cur = jit(cur) }" dispatch loop shrinks to a thin entry + resume.

## Adds

- run_internal's dispatch loop short-circuits: when entered with a non-parking fn, run it to completion (it returns the program's final value via the native call chain) and stop. Only re-enter the loop for parking events.
- Halt path: a non-parking fn's chain reaches Term::Halt; the chain returns the halt value up the native stack; run_internal records it.

## Kills

- The central trampoline dispatch loop's per-call iteration for non-parking call chains (the bulk of trampoline traffic in non-concurrent programs).
- Per-tick scheduler ticks for non-parking paths.

## Acceptance

- Matrix green.
- Non-concurrent fixtures execute without entering the trampoline loop body (loop fires once for entry, exits when halt is reached).
- concurrency_ping_pong still works (parking-reachable paths keep using the loop).

## Depends on

- fz-ul4.27.6.3

## Blocks

- fz-ul4.27.8 (VR.6: stack frames + return_call TCO)
title: VR.4.4: trampoline retirement for non-parking paths
